cycle_label: GOV-10 — governance-thread standalone sprint · consolidation pass (action-surface discredited-mechanism scan · canonical-source/surface-idempotency audit · shared-engine pattern audit) · runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade

plain_english._doc: Operator-facing summary block. /brief reads this to emit a plain-language header. The governance thread is NOT a cycle — it is a thread of standalone GOV-NN sprints. cycle_number tracks the GOV sprint number (GOV-10 = 10). Re-author this block at each GOV sprint OPEN.
plain_english.summary: GOV-10 CLOSED 2026-05-15. First deliberately multi-loop GOV sprint since GOV-06 — bundled four independent governance threads sharing the same root discipline (a fact codified in memory but not propagated to its surfaces has only half-landed). Same-day open-to-close, all four loops clean. **L01** fresh sweep: zero failed units, composite 64 (↑6 since GOV-07), reconciliation_signal 72 holding, no open HITLs. **L02** action-surface audit: 14+ surfaces inventoried across casey-junior backend + darntech frontend + cross-sprint-audit, ZERO discredited prescriptions found, GOV-07's fix held cleanly, the old 'Register unmapped repos in PROJECT_ENDPOINTS' phrasing survives only in historical sprint-logs and memory (appropriate); generic-applicability question recorded as answered in [[fix-without-action-surface-reconciliation]]. **L03** canonical-source audit (matches operator's surface-idempotency framing): 3 facts examined (casey-junior prod endpoint, rag-core-client deployed state, N1/N2 bifurcation); endpoint + bifurcation are clean across all surfaces; rag-core-client state surfaced TWO LIVE DRIFTS — canonical source pyproject says v0.4.0 but prod casey-junior venv has v0.3.0 (silent env-var bridge break: systemd unit sets RAG_CORE_URL but v0.3.0 code reads DARNTECH_RAG_URL, works only because defaults match), AND GOV-09 close note claiming 'GOVERNANCE_RAG_URL deployed to prod casey-junior venv' is provably stale — /proc/$pid/environ doesn't have it (function works via hardcoded default which happens to match the right instance); both flagged carry-forward as operator-review (behavior-changing prod work). New memory file [[canonical-source-per-fact]] codifies the audit pattern + records the L03 n=3 worked example. **L04** shared-engine pattern audit: rag-core confirmed as the ONLY shared-engine-multi-instance pattern in the homelab; all 3 instances (darntech-rag :8000, dellatech-rag :8001, governance-rag :8002) correctly self-identify with engine + instance disambiguation + data-layer signals; the discipline from [[engine-identity-at-instance-health]] is fully applied. The audit demonstrably worked: L03 surfaced real drift that single-surface reading wouldn't catch (sprint-log + systemd + venv triangle disagreed in two places — only cross-grep revealed it).
goals: L01 — fresh health-check sweep per GOV-01 operating model. CLOSED — sweep clean, queue confirmed

L02 — action-surface discredited-mechanism scan (GOV-07 carryforward). CLOSED — 14+ surfaces audited, ZERO discredited prescriptions, GOV-07 question recorded as answered in [[fix-without-action-surface-reconciliation]]

L03 — canonical-source / surface-idempotency audit. CLOSED — 3 facts audited, 2 live drifts on prod casey-junior surfaced + flagged carry-forward, new memory [[canonical-source-per-fact]] codifies the discipline
L04 — shared-engine pattern audit. CLOSED — rag-core confirmed sole homelab shared-engine pattern, all 3 instances disambiguated, [[engine-identity-at-instance-health]] discipline fully applied
plain_english.proposed_next_action: GOV-10 is CLOSED. Net for the sprint: 1 new governance memory file ([[canonical-source-per-fact]]) + 2 audit-completion addenda to existing memories (fix-without-action-surface-reconciliation + engine-identity-at-instance-health) + 2 live drifts surfaced on prod casey-junior for operator review. Carryforward for GOV-11 (or operator-direct): (1) rag-core-client v0.4.0 redeploy across consumers (casey-junior + lorna-financials + ellabot) — same shape as RC6 rename, operator-review gate; (2) GOVERNANCE_RAG_URL persistence policy decision — persist to systemd EnvironmentFile or accept default-as-canonical; (3) reconciler-confidence-deployment-scoped structural fix still uncommitted in casey-junior/app/services/reconciler.py — operator-review gate; (4) 24 still-pending reconciliation suggestions across 8 deployments — operator-review work; (5) ReconciliationPanel rework retry — operator decision; (6) broader memory canonicalization sweep (where-does-memory-live across all projects) — too broad for one sprint, future GOV. GOV-11 opens from a fresh sweep when next scheduled. Standing instruments unchanged.

cycle_mode_notes: GOV sprints do not pillar-rotate and do not join the cascade. cascade_mode is standalone by design — the three-sprint cascade is capped at 3 (three is the floor AND the ceiling). GOV takes ownerless cross-cutting work, never nephew COLLECT-queue work.
structure: Health-check sweep scopes the queue → GOV executes the ownerless backlog as a standalone sprint → findings codified to memory + DAcumen
cascade_mode: standalone — runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade, not a 4th nephew
work_locus: casey-junior + darntech (action surfaces) · cross-repo (canonical-source audit) · cross-instance (shared-engine audit)
authored_by: operator-direct — GOV-10 opened 2026-05-15 same-day after GOV-09 close, on operator framing: 'what would be some other consolidation style or governance work we should be working on? could be document codification a/ alignment, surface idempotency for specific bits of info across notion / obsidian / frontend doc pages etc or something else'. Operator selected the multi-loop bundling explicitly ('stuff whatever we can in the gov-10 charter responsibly with full plans or whatever ensures success then we'll just run GOV-10 that way'). The three loops were scoped from candidate enumeration: action-surface scan (GOV-07 carryforward, named in GOV-09 close), canonical-source audit (matches operator's surface-idempotency framing), shared-engine audit (engine-identity memory's generic applicability). Items explicitly held back: reconciler structural fix (operator-review gate), pending-suggestions walk (operator work, not GOV), panel rework retry (no operator signal), broader memory canonicalization (too broad).
stakes: Same family as GOV-07 and GOV-08: memory codifies a discipline; the surfaces that use the data must embody it. GOV-07 enforced this at one action surface (`evidence_coverage`); GOV-08 enforced it at one storage surface (the suggestions array). GOV-10 generalizes: every dashboard action surface gets the GOV-07 audit; high-traffic facts that appear on multiple surfaces get a named canonical source; shared engines get the disambiguation discipline applied wherever they're deployed. If the consolidation pass works, GOV's memory files become enforced disciplines, not just documentation. If a loop turns up new failures the cascade can't absorb, those become next-sprint scope.
notes: GOV-10 is the first deliberately multi-loop GOV sprint since GOV-06 (which had L01-L04 across the GOV-06 thread). L02-L04 are independent — each can close without the others. Standing instruments unchanged since GOV-07 sweep (doc-health-check.timer 08:15 + observatory-doc-health-snapshot.timer 23:50 + governance-rag-indexer.timer 02:31 + governance-rag instance #3 at 192.168.0.151:8002 verified working from prod casey-junior since GOV-09). Related memory: fix-without-action-surface-reconciliation (L02 generalizes it), denormalization-staleness-pattern (parallel structural shape), engine-identity-at-instance-health (L04 generalizes it), reconciler-suggestion-confidence-deployment-scoped (held back from scope).
